skill.name: i18n-localization
skill.description: Guides internationalization work by finding hardcoded strings, checking locale files, and planning RTL support for web and Python apps.
skill.summary: Review applications for translation readiness, locale completeness, and RTL layout needs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static high findings for certificate files and weak cryptography are false positives. The flagged lines contain localization key handling, Markdown examples, and front matter text, not secrets or cryptographic code. The included Python checker reads local project files, so publication is acceptable with a filesystem warning.

medium_findings.title: Local Project File Scanning Script
medium_findings.description: The Python checker accepts a project path, discovers locale and code files, and reads file contents. This is expected for an i18n audit, but it can inspect sensitive local projects if run broadly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly performs recursive local file discovery and reads files from a user-supplied path. No network or shell execution evidence was found, so the concern is bounded to local filesystem exposure.

medium_findings.title: Documented User-Invoked Script Command
medium_findings.description: The skill documents a command that runs the bundled Python checker. This is a legitimate helper command, but marketplace users should review the script before running it on private repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and invokes a local script with a project path. It is not hidden or automatically executed by the skill instructions.

low_findings.title: Certificate Key Static Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged lines that handle translation key sets and language names. These lines do not contain certificate files, private keys, or secret material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code compares locale key names across languages. There is no certificate path, private key literal, or credential handling at these lines.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged the skill description line, but that text describes localization patterns. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML front matter describing i18n and localization. The finding has no semantic connection to cryptography.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Most flagged backticks are Markdown code fences for React, Next.js, Python, CSS, and file structure examples. They are documentation examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside Markdown fenced blocks and contains framework examples. The only actual command is separately assessed as a user-invoked helper script.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Local File Reads
dangerous_patterns.description: The checker recursively discovers project files and reads matching files to detect i18n issues. This behavior is expected, but it should be disclosed because it can process private source text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code uses recursive globbing and file reads against the target path. The pattern is clear, but no evidence shows data leaves the local process.

content.value_statement: Internationalization gaps cause missing translations, broken layouts, and inconsistent locale behavior. This skill guides audits for hardcoded strings, locale files, formatting, and RTL support.
actual_capabilities: Explains i18n and localization concepts for application teams.
Shows React, Next.js, and Python translation patterns.
Recommends locale file organization by language and namespace.
Checks for hardcoded strings with the included Python script.
Checks locale key completeness across JSON translation files.
Provides RTL layout guidance using CSS logical properties.
limitations: The checker uses pattern matching and can miss dynamic UI strings.
It does not translate content or verify translation quality.
It scans only selected file types and common locale paths.
It does not replace native i18n library tests or QA review.

use_cases.title: Prepare a SaaS App for New Markets
use_cases.description: Review UI strings, locale file structure, date formatting, and RTL requirements before adding new languages.

use_cases.title: Add Translation Checks to a Code Review
use_cases.description: Use the checklist and helper script to find hardcoded strings and missing locale keys before release.

use_cases.title: Improve Accessibility for RTL Users
use_cases.description: Identify layout assumptions and update styles with logical properties for right-to-left languages.

prompt_templates.title: Review My App for i18n Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project for basic internationalization readiness. Focus on hardcoded strings, locale file structure, and missing fallback behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Check Translation Key Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my locale files and identify missing or extra translation keys. Summarize the affected languages and namespaces.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor UI Strings to Translation Keys
prompt_templates.prompt: Find user-facing strings in these components and suggest translation keys. Keep namespaces aligned with each feature area.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Advanced RTL Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an RTL readiness review for this application. Include layout risks, icon direction, form behavior, and locale formatting checks.

output_examples.input: A React page contains direct button labels and headings.
output: Hardcoded heading text found in the page component.
Button labels should move to translation keys.

output_examples.input: English and Turkish locale files have different keys.
output: Turkish locale is missing two keys from the English baseline.
One Turkish key has no matching English source key.
Review the auth namespace before release.
Replace left and right spacing with logical CSS properties.
Test icon direction under right-to-left layout.
Verify date, number, and plural formatting for Arabic.

best_practices: Use stable translation keys instead of raw user-facing strings.
Group locale files by feature so ownership is clear.
Test fallback language, plural rules, and RTL layouts before release.
anti_patterns: Do not concatenate translated fragments to build sentences.
Do not assume translated text has the same length as English.
Do not run the checker on broad private directories without review.

faq.question: Does this skill translate my application content?
faq.answer: No. It helps identify translation readiness issues and organize localization work.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it cover?
faq.answer: It includes examples for React, Next.js, Vue patterns, and Python gettext workflows.

faq.question: Can it find every hardcoded string?
faq.answer: No. The checker uses regular expressions and may miss dynamic strings or uncommon patterns.

faq.question: Does the script send data over the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The script reads local files and prints results to the console.

faq.question: Is it safe to run on private repositories?
faq.answer: Review the script first and run it only on the project path you intend to inspect.

faq.question: Does it support right-to-left languages?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides RTL guidance for layout, spacing, and directional icons.
